title: Committed review does not use helper scripts for trivial deterministic checks
description: A committed-review scenario where changed validation strings tempt the reviewer to run a helper command just to compute a string length or prove a trivial literal fact. The correct review should reason directly from the diff and file content.

scope.notes: Sanitized fixture models a changed managed-certificate hostname validation path where the reviewer only needs direct reasoning about literal lengths and validation layering.

mustCatch.description: The review should reason directly from the diff and file content for trivial deterministic facts like string length rather than running helper scripts or terminal calculations.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not run ad hoc shell snippets, PowerShell expressions, or helper scripts just to measure a changed literal or other trivial deterministic fact.

notes: Use this case to benchmark the no-helper-script rule for trivial deterministic review checks.
